page: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture and managed via the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores with 8 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It does not utilize hyperthreading, meaning each vCPU maps directly to a dedicated physical core. The processor is equipped with 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The system is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with the Geekbench single-core score of 1890.0 and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.31 GB/sec both ranking in the top 25% tier. Text processing performance is also strong, scoring 2348.0 in Geekbench. The Passmark memory score of 3082.18 places the instance in the top 10% tier for memory performance. However, multi-core and general computational benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the average category. Notably, the Passmark CPU encryption test scored 1504.19, placing it in the bottom 25% tier, and prompt processing for a medium 7B LLM model registered at a poor 15.42 tokens/sec, which is in the bottom 10% tier.

The m8g.2xlarge instance presents a balanced resource profile for general-purpose applications, offering high memory bandwidth and dedicated core execution. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable performance per vCPU, though the lack of local NVMe storage and hardware accelerators limits its utility for storage-heavy or GPU-dependent tasks. Qualitatively, the instance provides a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high memory throughput and steady CPU performance without the premium of specialized hardware. It is suitable for application servers, web serving, database operations, and lightweight text or data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, a 4 Gbps baseline network speed, and no local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core execution and top-tier memory performance, highlighted by a Passmark memory score in the top 10% tier. However, it exhibits weaker performance in encryption and medium LLM prompt processing. The instance offers a balanced, cost-efficient profile for general-purpose workloads, making it suitable for web serving, database operations, and text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is an ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 32 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.2xlarge is an 8-core ARM general-purpose instance with 32 GB RAM, offering strong single-core and memory performance.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance delivering dedicated physical cores, high memory bandwidth, and balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier memory performance ranking in the top 10% of benchmarks
Strong single-core CPU and text processing execution
No local storage or GPU accelerators with 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications
